_meta.note: All rule fields are structured AST objects. No pseudo-code strings. Every node is machine-traversable.

date: DIFF_DAYS — if from > to, the circuit produces a deterministic denial (rad: SYSTEM_DATE_ERROR, route: PENDING_MANUAL_REVIEW) instead of computing the difference. Never produces an unprovable proof.

_meta.field_ref_convention: Bare string = field on shared_context. Object { ref: 'x.y' } = nested path. Object { literal: v } = constant value.
_meta.hash_scheme_note: Poseidon used for all in-circuit hashing (BabyJubJub-friendly, ~600 constraints/hash). keccak256 used only for off-circuit attestation_hash fields (~100k constraints — never inside proof).
_meta.signature_scheme_note: EdDSA on BabyJubJub for all oracle signatures. DHCS/CalHHS oracle public keys are hardcoded in circuit as constants.
_meta.date_encoding: All dates encoded as integer days since 1970-01-01 (proleptic Gregorian). No Unix timestamps. ISO-8601 fields converted by relayer before circuit ingestion. DIFF_DAYS(from, to): if from > to, the circuit immediately produces a deterministic denial (rad: SYSTEM_DATE_ERROR, route: PENDING_MANUAL_REVIEW, appeal_eligible: false) before attempting the calculation. An unprovable proof is never an acceptable output — every input combination must produce either a valid proof or a deterministic denial. The relayer also guarantees from <= to ordering before injection as defense-in-depth, but the circuit guard is authoritative.
_meta.attestation_hash_note: attestation_hash is a Poseidon commitment computed inside the circuit. keccak256 is never used inside the proof. The relayer relays the Poseidon output on-chain as a cheap commitment. No keccak256 in any output schema.
_meta.nullifier_concurrency_model: Sequential proof verification with snapshot root. Prover fetches Merkle witness against root R_n and embeds R_n as a public input. On-chain verifier rejects proof if current_root != R_n. Concurrent claim approval advances root to R_n+1 — prover must refetch witness and resubmit. ZK proof generation (2-10s today) is the throughput bottleneck, so sequential verification does not meaningfully reduce throughput. Revisit batch verification when hardware provers (FPGA/ASIC) bring proof time below 200ms.
_meta.leaf_canonicalization: All Poseidon leaf inputs derived from string fields (provider_type, procedure_code, etc.) are encoded as 32-byte arrays: left-justified, null-padded to 32 bytes (0x00). Circomlib/Aztec convention. Relayer and circuit MUST use identical encoding. Required for deterministic test vectors.

public_input_usage.snapshot_root_epoch: Relayer root freshness check — verified off-circuit before proof submission

_meta.circuit_constraint_estimate: ~9-15 EdDSA BabyJubJub signature verifications (~100k constraints each) = ~900k-1.5M + ~30 Poseidon hashes (~600 constraints each) = ~18k + ~1 oracle key registry Merkle inclusion (~20k) + ~2 nullifier SMT non-membership proofs (~40k each) = ~80k + ~150 comparisons/arithmetic nodes (~5-20 constraints each) = ~3k = ~2.5-3M total constraints. Fits comfortably in Plonkish provers (Halo2, Plonky2) with 128 GB RAM. GPU proving time <30s (RTX 4090 class). 32-core CPU: ~4-5 min. Note: 9 oracles minimum; up to ~15 if ALJ override and sub-oracle calls are included. Re-evaluate if oracle count exceeds 20 or nullifier tree depth exceeds 32.

key_rotation_model.problem: Hardcoded oracle pubkeys require full circuit redeploy on key compromise or expiry.
key_rotation_model.solution: On-chain key registry verified inside circuit as an additional Merkle inclusion proof.

registry_architecture.structure: Mapping: oracle_id => { pubkey: bytes32, valid_from: uint64, valid_thru: uint64, revoked: bool }
registry_architecture.merkle_root: Registry committed as Merkle tree root, published on-chain each epoch

leaf.canonicalization: All inputs zero-padded to 32 bytes. See _meta.leaf_canonicalization.

circuit_verification.note: Depth 8 = 256 oracle slots. More than sufficient. ~20k additional constraints per claim.

rotation_procedure: 1. New key registered in OracleKeyRegistry.sol with valid_from = next_epoch
3. Relayer begins signing with new key at valid_from epoch
4. Old key marked revoked — circuit rejects proofs using revoked key
key_rotation_model.constraint_cost: ~20k additional constraints per claim (1 Merkle inclusion proof, depth 8). Negligible.
key_rotation_model.pilot_note: For production, all oracle public keys should be stored in an on-chain registry with Merkle inclusion proofs (see key_rotation_model.registry_architecture). Hardcoded keys are acceptable only for pilot/test environments.

execution_model.description: Five-layer sequential gate. Layer N+1 is unreachable unless layer N writes status PASS into shared_context.

provider_number.note: NPI or legacy Medi-Cal provider number

ccn_original.note: 13-digit Claim Control Number from original paid claim

provider_procedure_table_root.note: Merkle root of CA-MMIS Provider/Procedure Authorization Table. Public input. Versioned and signed by CA-MMIS oracle key. Updated when table changes.
provider_procedure_table_root.cardinality_note: Depth 20 = 2^20 = 1,048,576 slots. CA-MMIS table is sparse — actual valid (provider_type, procedure_code) pairings estimated in the tens of thousands. Pre-deployment: relayer MUST validate actual cardinality against live CA-MMIS data. If cardinality approaches 500k entries, upgrade to depth 24 (16M slots) before deployment.

o9_certification_date.note: Days since 1970-01-01. Date certification was signed.

claim_received_date_adjusted.note: Days since 1970-01-01. Injected by off-circuit workflow engine when E8 toll applies. Overrides claim_received_date for E6 evaluation. null = no toll applied.

o7_determination.note: Injected by workflow engine on resubmission after O7 oracle fires. null on first submission.

pend_start_epoch.note: Unix epoch seconds. Set by relayer when circuit outputs route=PENDING_CLINICAL_REVIEW (E8). Stored off-circuit. Used to compute toll_days for E6 timely filing on resubmission.

L1_ORACLE_INJECTION.purpose: Inject all external oracle payloads into shared_context before any gate logic runs. Each oracle defines its source, input, output fields, TTL, and stale-response failure behavior.

signature_verification.on_fail: REJECT_PAYLOAD — do not inject into shared_context

on_stale_or_failed.note: Graceful degradation. System does not freeze on oracle unavailability.
on_stale_or_failed.on_sla_breach: DENY with RAD 001 and provider notification

O7_MEDICAL_REVIEW_DETERMINATION.note: Single-use oracle. Consumed once by E8 reentry path, then invalidated.

message.canonicalization: All string inputs zero-padded to 32 bytes (left-justified, null-padded 0x00). See _meta.leaf_canonicalization.

signature_verification.on_fail: REJECT_PAYLOAD — do not inject into shared_context

on_stale_or_failed.on_sla_breach: DENY with RAD 019 and provider notification

O9_PHYSICIAN_CERTIFICATION.source_system: DHCS or facility-submitted physician certification of medical necessity
O9_PHYSICIAN_CERTIFICATION.trigger_condition: facility_type IN [NF_A, NF_B] AND DIFF_DAYS(date_of_service_thru, date_of_service_from) > 30

message.canonicalization: All string inputs zero-padded to 32 bytes (left-justified, null-padded 0x00). See _meta.leaf_canonicalization.

signature_verification.on_fail: REJECT_PAYLOAD — do not inject into shared_context

on_stale_or_failed.on_sla_breach: DENY with RAD 038 and provider notification

relayer_architecture.purpose: Off-circuit indexed relayer pulls signed oracle payloads on TTL schedule. Proof generation uses cached signed data — no live API call inside circuit.
responsibilities: Pull and verify 9 oracle payloads (O1–O9) on their individual TTL schedules
Verify EdDSA signature from source system before caching
Serve signed payloads + Merkle witnesses to prover on request
Maintain Nullifier Tree (Sparse Merkle Tree depth=32)
Insert new nullifiers atomically on every APPROVED claim
Publish current nullifier_tree_root as on-chain commitment

nullifier_tree.root_commitment: published on-chain — public input to every proof

relayer_architecture.failure_isolation: Oracle outage affects only claims requiring that oracle. Other claims continue processing.

L2_ELIGIBILITY_PROOF.purpose: Gate layer. All oracles must be VERIFIED. Produces eligibility_attestation with bytes32 hash for ZK circuit.

G7_ASSET_TEST.citation: ACWDL 26-02 — asset_limit_usd sourced from this document, overrides Article 9/10

G9_RECIPIENT_DEATH_CHECK.note: Recipient must be alive on date of service. Separate from provider death (L3 G5).

on_fail.note: Recipient deceased before date of service. No appeal rights — clinical impossibility.

attestation_hash.value: Poseidon(status, aid_code, benefit_level, soc_cleared, asset_test_passed, disability_verified, eligibility_verified_ts)
attestation_hash.note: Computed inside circuit. Relayed on-chain by relayer. Never keccak256.

L3_PROVIDER_ENROLLMENT.purpose: Verify all providers are enrolled, active, and not suspended on the date of service.

G3_PROVIDER_TYPE_SERVICE_MATCH.implementation_note: CA-MMIS authorization table is ~15,000 procedure codes x ~200 provider types. Too large for Plookup directly. Table is committed as a Merkle tree. Root published on-chain as provider_procedure_table_root public input. Relayer serves Merkle inclusion witness for (provider_type, procedure_code) pair.

leaf.canonicalization: Both inputs zero-padded to 32 bytes (left-justified, null-padded 0x00). Example: 'NF_A' → [0x4E,0x46,0x5F,0x41,0x00,...,0x00] (32 bytes). Matches _meta.leaf_canonicalization. Required for test vector parity between relayer and circuit.

attestation_hash.note: Computed inside circuit. Relayed on-chain by relayer. Never keccak256.

L4_CLAIMS_TAR_ADJUDICATION.purpose: Full adjudication edit layer. Hard prerequisite: eligibility and provider attestations must both be PASS.

E1_DUPLICATE_DETECTION.implementation_note: Replaces database ALL_MATCH scan. Nullifier tree maintained by relayer. O(log n) witness, not O(n) scan.

nullifier_construction.canonicalization: All string inputs zero-padded to 32 bytes (left-justified, null-padded 0x00). See _meta.leaf_canonicalization.

on_approve_side_effect.instruction: Relayer inserts claim_nullifier into Nullifier Tree after APPROVED status is written. nullifier_tree_root updated atomically.
on_approve_side_effect.atomicity_requirement: Payment release and nullifier insertion must be atomic. Rollback both on failure.

E2_TAR_REQUIRED.note: PENDING TAR routes to manual hold (may approve before filing window closes). EXPIRED TAR is hard deny. Only APPROVED passes.

PENDING.note: TAR may be approved before timely filing deadline. Hold claim, re-evaluate on TAR status change.

EXPIRED.note: TAR expired before date of service. Provider must obtain new TAR and resubmit.

DENIED.note: TAR was explicitly denied. Appeal TAR denial first, then resubmit claim.

E3_TAR_RECIPIENT_MATCH.citation: RAD_Repository-1.xlsx RAD Code 004 CARC 284 RARC N54

E4_TAR_DATE_RANGE.citation: RAD_Repository-1.xlsx RAD Code 006 CARC 284 RARC N351

E5_TAR_CLAIM_CORRESPONDENCE.citation: RAD_Repository-1.xlsx RAD Code 035 CARC 284 RARC N54

E6_TIMELY_FILING.citation: RAD_Repository-1.xlsx RAD Code 021 CARC 29 | elect.pdf 6-month resubmission window

frequency_type_7_exception.description: Replacement claims: RAD date of prior claim must be within 6 months of resubmission

toll_during_pend.description: v7.2.0: Toll computed off-circuit by workflow engine. Circuit uses claim_received_date_adjusted when non-null.
toll_during_pend.note: The toll_days value is supplied by the relayer as claim_received_date_adjusted — a private input to the proof that includes O7. The circuit does not remember state from a previous proof. The relayer computes the toll off-circuit using floor((o7_completed_at_epoch - recorded_pend_start_epoch) / 86400), then injects the adjusted date before proof generation. Floor division is authoritative — relayer implementation MUST use integer floor, not rounding.

E7_ONCE_PER_DOS.citation: RAD_Repository-1.xlsx RAD Code 029 CARC 119 RARC M86
E7_ONCE_PER_DOS.implementation_note: Narrower nullifier than E1 — covers recipient+procedure+DOS only. Uses same Nullifier Tree.

nullifier_construction.canonicalization: All string inputs zero-padded to 32 bytes (left-justified, null-padded 0x00). See _meta.leaf_canonicalization.

on_approve_side_effect.instruction: Relayer inserts once_per_dos_nullifier into Nullifier Tree after APPROVED.
on_approve_side_effect.note: E1 and E7 nullifiers are distinct hashes — both inserted on approval, both checked independently.

E8_SUBACUTE_CONFLICT.architecture_note: v7.2.0: HALT/reentry state machine moved OFF-CIRCUIT. Statefulness in ZK circuits is operationally dangerous: pend_start_epoch injection errors and toll floor-division consensus splits are silent failure modes. NEW MODEL: Circuit outputs PENDING_CLINICAL_REVIEW as a terminal result. A traditional stateful workflow engine (off-chain) handles O7 response and resubmission. The resubmission arrives as a NEW claim with o7_determination injected as a signed oracle payload. E6 timely filing toll is computed by the workflow engine and injected as claim_received_date_adjusted. This removes all HALT state, pend_start_epoch, l4_halt_residual_flags, and reentry logic from the circuit.

off_circuit_workflow.step_2: Routes to Medical Review Unit queue (SLA: 72 hours)
off_circuit_workflow.step_3: O7 oracle payload signed on determination
off_circuit_workflow.step_4: Workflow engine computes adjusted_received_date accounting for pend duration
off_circuit_workflow.step_5: Resubmission injected as new claim with o7_determination in shared_context
off_circuit_workflow.step_6: New proof generated — E8 trigger evaluates differently with o7 present

on_trigger.note: The relayer records the current Unix time as pend_start_epoch when the circuit outputs status = HALT. This value is stored off-circuit by the relayer and used later when O7 arrives. The circuit itself is stateless — it does not set or remember pend_start_epoch.
on_trigger.appeal_track_note: rad stays '036' for Track A selector compatibility. The off-circuit workflow engine reads the 'route' field (PENDING_CLINICAL_REVIEW) to determine queue routing. The L5 Track selector uses the rad code '036' to route to Track A (CIF resubmission) on final denial.

ACUTE_CONFIRMED.action: claim_type_code overridden to ACUTE_INPATIENT_CONFIRMED by workflow engine before injection

SUBACUTE_CONFIRMED.action: CLOSE — workflow engine marks original as RECLASSIFIED

INSUFFICIENT_DOCUMENTATION.action: DENY RAD 036 RARC N366 — appeal eligible

single_halt_invariant.statelessness_note: All state tracking (pend_start_epoch, toll calculation) is off-circuit, managed by the relayer. The circuit remains stateless across proofs. The relayer computes toll_days = floor((o7_epoch - pend_start_epoch) / 86400) off-circuit and injects the result as claim_received_date_adjusted before the resubmission proof.
single_halt_invariant.future_engineer_warning: If a second HALT path is ever added, the relayer MUST track multiple (start, end) intervals and E6 toll MUST sum all intervals. Do NOT add a second HALT without updating the relayer toll computation logic. All state tracking remains off-circuit.

G10_PHYSICIAN_CERTIFICATION.note: NF-A/NF-B stays >30 days require physician certification of medical necessity. Date-order guard at top: if from > to, immediate SYSTEM_DATE_ERROR denial — never computes DIFF_DAYS on invalid inputs. Null-safe: o9_certification_date IS_NOT_NULL checked before LTE comparison.

else.note: date_of_service_from > date_of_service_thru — deterministic denial. Never attempts DIFF_DAYS on invalid inputs.

on_fail.note: Provider must submit physician certification retroactively via CIF resubmission.

status.note: HALT removed in v7.2.2. E8 now outputs DENY with rad=036 and route=PENDING_CLINICAL_REVIEW. All stateful HALT tracking is handled off-circuit by the relayer/workflow engine.

L5_APPEAL_EXCEPTION.purpose: Denial → appeal → override. Activated when l4_adjudication_result.appeal_eligible == true.

TRACK_A_CIF_RESUBMISSION.citation: RAD_Repository-1.xlsx RAD Code 004 billing tips | elect.pdf 6-month window

outcomes.DENY_FINAL_NO_FURTHER_APPEAL: Write final denial to 835 with MA130

outcomes.APPROVE_OVERRIDE: Re-enter L4 with override_flag = true — skip re-evaluation of overridden gate
outcomes.PARTIAL_APPROVAL_ADJUSTED: Re-enter L4 with adjusted claim values

appeal_override_schema.description: Override flag is cryptographically bound to one specific gate on one specific claim. Cannot be generalized or replayed.

gate_id_hash.canonicalization: All string inputs zero-padded to 32 bytes (left-justified, null-padded 0x00). See _meta.leaf_canonicalization.

message.canonicalization: All string inputs zero-padded to 32 bytes (left-justified, null-padded 0x00). See _meta.leaf_canonicalization.

authorized_by.on_fail: REJECT_OVERRIDE — claim remains DENIED, malicious prover cannot bypass arbitrary gates

appeal_override_schema.security_note: A malicious prover cannot use override_flag=true to skip timely filing, duplicate detection, or any gate not explicitly named in the ALJ signed attestation. Each override is a unique Poseidon commitment of (gate, claim, epoch) — cannot be replayed.

outcomes.HOLD_SUSTAINED: DENY — refer for potential exclusion proceedings

terminal_denial.citation: RAD_Repository-1.xlsx RARC MA130 — no appeal rights afforded on unprocessable claims
terminal_denial.note: RAD 010 (duplicate), 019 (deceased recipient), 021 (timely filing), 030 (provider deceased) — all non-appealable terminal denials.

rust_interface.oracle_attestation_struct: pub struct OracleAttestation { pub oracle_id: [u8; 32], pub payload_hash: [u8; 32], pub injected_at_epoch: u64, pub expiry_epoch: u64, pub signature: [u8; 64], pub status: u8 }
rust_interface.nullifier_witness_struct: pub struct MerkleWitness { pub leaf: [u8; 32], pub path: Vec<[u8; 32]>, pub indices: Vec<bool> }
rust_interface.poseidon_note: Use poseidon_rs crate or arkworks-rs poseidon. BabyJubJub-parameterized. Do NOT use keccak256 inside circuit.

new_structs.AljOverrideAttestation: pub struct AljOverrideAttestation { pub gate_id_hash: [u8; 32], pub claim_id: [u8; 32], pub override_epoch: u64, pub signature: [u8; 64] }
new_structs.ClaimContext_date_note: All date fields encoded as i32 (integer days since 1970-01-01). Relayer converts ISO-8601 to this before proof generation.

_manifest_meta.purpose: Acceptance criteria for circuit implementation. One PASS + one FAIL per gate.
_manifest_meta.convention: All dates as integer days since 1970-01-01. DOS_FROM=19723 = 2024-01-01. DOS_FROM=19754 = 2024-02-01.

FAIL.description: Recipient ID not found in MMIS — inactive
Aid code 01 is not a valid LTC code
Date of service outside eligibility period
SOC of $150 required but not yet met
Aid code 53 requires NF_A/NF_B but facility is RCFE
Recipient age 70 fails 21-64 constraint for aid code 53
Assets $3000 exceed $2000 limit with no partnership disregard
Aid code 6J requires active disability but determination expired

PASS.description: Recipient alive — service date before date of death

FAIL.description: Recipient deceased before date of service — clinical impossibility
Billing provider not enrolled in Medi-Cal on date of service
Provider on CalHHS suspended and ineligible list
Provider type not authorized for procedure code

PASS.description: First submission — nullifier not in tree

FAIL.description: Identical claim already paid — nullifier in tree

HOLD_PENDING.description: TAR pending — claim held in manual queue

FAIL.description: Claim received more than 365 days after date of service

PASS.description: Acute claim at acute facility — no conflict

PENDING_CLINICAL_REVIEW.description: Subacute facility + ACUTE_INPATIENT code — routes to clinical review queue

expected.note: No HALT state in circuit. Workflow engine handles queue routing off-circuit.

PASS.description: NF-A stay >30 days with valid physician certification on file

expected.note: DIFF_DAYS=32 > 30, cert on file and predates DOS

FAIL.description: NF-A stay >30 days but no physician certification on file

SKIP.description: NF-A stay <=30 days — gate skips, no certification required

BOUNDARY_AND_ADVERSARIAL._note: Production-grade coverage. Run these AFTER gate acceptance vectors pass.

EXACTLY_30_DAYS_NO_CERT_REQUIRED.description: Exactly 30 days — G10 condition is GT 30, so 30 days does NOT trigger cert requirement

EXACTLY_31_DAYS_CERT_REQUIRED.description: Exactly 31 days — triggers cert requirement. No cert on file.

EXACTLY_365_DAYS_PASS.description: Received on day 365 exactly — must PASS (LTE 365)

DUPLICATE_EXACT_MATCH.description: Identical claim resubmitted — nullifier already in tree
DUPLICATE_EXACT_MATCH.precondition: Nullifier for this combination already inserted in SMT

INVALID_WITNESS_REJECTED.description: Valid nullifier non-membership claimed but witness is corrupted — proof must fail to generate

expected.note: Circuit constraint violation — prover cannot produce valid proof

REPLAYED_SIGNATURE.description: Valid signature from O1 replayed with different payload — must reject
REPLAYED_SIGNATURE.attack: Attacker replaces payload bytes but reuses signature from a prior valid oracle call

expected.note: EdDSA non-malleability: signature binds to exact Poseidon(oracle_id, payload_bytes, injected_at_epoch)

WRONG_ORACLE_KEY.description: Payload signed by O2 key presented as O1 payload

expected.note: oracle_key_registry_root inclusion proof will fail — O2 key not registered for O1 oracle_id

SWAPPED_DATES_DETERMINISTIC_DENY.description: date_of_service_thru < date_of_service_from — must produce deterministic DENY, never UNDEFINED

expected.note: ASSERT(to >= from) fires before DIFF_DAYS. Never an unprovable hole.

SWAPPED_DATES_DETERMINISTIC_DENY.note: Matches _meta.date_encoding and G10 else-branch rad: SYSTEM_DATE_ERROR.

ORACLE_EXACTLY_AT_TTL_BOUNDARY.description: O1 oracle payload injected exactly 24 hours ago — must still be valid (LTE not LT)
ORACLE_EXACTLY_AT_TTL_BOUNDARY.note: Delta = exactly 86400 seconds = 24 hours. Valid if TTL is LTE 24h.

ORACLE_ONE_SECOND_STALE.description: O1 oracle payload injected 24 hours + 1 second ago — must be stale
